macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $100/Barrel Amid Iran Conflict Disruptions
items.summary: Oil prices spiked with WTI crude exceeding $104 and Brent reaching $120 due to Iran war disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on Qatar’s LNG facilities, driving U.S. gas prices above $4/gallon and diesel over $8 in San Francisco.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell Warns of Energy Supply Shock from Surging Oil and Gas Prices
items.summary: Jerome Powell cautioned about a new energy supply shock as oil tops $100/barrel and gas prices rise over 34% in a month, while Fed's John Williams notes potential inflation spread across sectors.

items.title: Inflation Expectations Spike to 5% on Iran War Oil Shock
items.summary: Short-term inflation expectations hit 5% per TIPS market data due to ongoing Iran conflict and energy disruptions, with forecasts of CPI reaching 4-5% in next three months unless war ends soon.

items.title: Fed Vice Chair Bowman Pencils in Three Rate Cuts by End of 2026
items.summary: Michelle Bowman anticipates three federal funds rate cuts before 2026 ends amid labor market concerns, while short-term markets price little rate movement over next 12 months.

items.title: Stocks Close Mixed as Trump Vows Hard Response to Iran; Dow Up 1,000 Points Earlier
items.summary: U.S. stocks ended mixed after Trump's vow to hit Iran 'extremely hard,' with Dow closing up 1,000 points on March 31 amid signals of possible de-escalation; oil soared over 10%.

items.title: JPMorgan CEO Warns Iran War Could Reignite Inflation, Keep Fed Rates Higher Longer
items.summary: JPMorgan's CEO stated the Iran war risks reigniting inflation, potentially forcing the Fed to maintain higher rates for an extended period.

items.title: Resilient U.S. Consumer and Labor Data Despite Energy Price Rise
items.summary: February retail sales rose 0.6% MoM exceeding expectations, private sector added 62,000 jobs vs. 40,000 forecast, with stable wage growth ahead of energy spike.

items.title: Airlines Hike Baggage Fees Amid Jet Fuel Surge from Iran War
items.summary: United raises checked bag fees by $10 and JetBlue by up to $9, first increases in years, citing soaring jet fuel costs linked to global oil shortages.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: TRU Token Surges 98% in 24 Hours with $178M Volume
items.summary: TRU led crypto gainers with a 98.46% surge to $0.0111, generating $177.78 million in trading volume, while RED followed with 65.80% gains to $0.1715. On the downside, ARIAIP plunged 49.24% to $0.0037 amid market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits $69K, Triggers $200M Short Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 3.3% to $69,341 amid thin liquidity in the Asia session, pushing above $69,000 and causing $200 million in short liquidations despite downplayed war tensions with Iran.

items.title: Analyst Predicts Bitcoin 'God Candle' to $1M
items.summary: Onchain analyst Willy Woo forecasts Bitcoin reaching over $1 million per coin in a future 'god candle' event, countering Peter Schiff's criticism of BTC's five-year performance.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Cuts OpenClaw Access from Claude Subscriptions
items.summary: Anthropic has ended OpenClaw access in Claude subscriptions amid clashes with Chinese AI rivals and a global token crunch, offering credits to ease user transition. This move impacts AI developers relying on the tool for building AI employees.

items.title: Foxconn Reports Record Q1 Sales Driven by AI Server Demand
items.summary: Foxconn achieved its highest-ever first-quarter sales of USD 66.62 billion, fueled by surging demand for AI servers. This reflects strong industry growth in AI infrastructure.

items.title: Windward Launches MAI Expert, First Generative AI Maritime Agent with AWS
items.summary: Windward, in partnership with AWS Generative AI Innovation Center, released MAI Expert on April 6, 2026, to enhance maritime anomaly analysis by combining geospatial intelligence and generative AI for rapid alert investigations.

items.title: KiloClaw Launched for Autonomous Agent Governance and Shadow AI Management
items.summary: KiloClaw was introduced on April 2, 2026, providing enterprises with tools to enforce governance over autonomous AI agents and address shadow AI risks.

items.title: OpenAI Advocates Robot Taxes and Public Wealth Fund for AI Job Losses
items.summary: OpenAI proposed robot taxes and a public wealth fund to mitigate job displacements caused by AI advancements.

items.title: Iran Threatens OpenAI’s $30B Stargate Data Center in UAE
items.summary: Iran has threatened to destroy OpenAI's $30 billion Stargate AI data center in Abu Dhabi, raising geopolitical tensions around AI infrastructure.

items.title: Japan Invests $6.3 Billion in Physical AI Due to Labor Shortages
items.summary: Japan is committing $6.3 billion to physical AI development, not to replace workers but to fill labor gaps amid demographic challenges.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 Delayed to May 2026
items.summary: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto VI from November 2026 to May 26, 2026, delivering anticipated bad news to fans awaiting the massive open-world title.

items.title: Starfield Launches on PlayStation 5
items.summary: Bethesda's sci-fi RPG Starfield, previously exclusive to PC and Xbox, arrives on PS5 on April 7, 2026, leveraging the console's hardware for a broader audience.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced and Immediately Playable
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo revealed Nioh 3 during Sony’s State of Play June showcase, allowing soulslike fans to play it right away.

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics Remake Revealed as The Ivalice Chronicles
items.summary: After months of rumors, a remake of the original PlayStation Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles was unveiled at PlayStation State of Play.

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Announced
items.summary: Silent Hill f received a new trailer and official release date following its debut at the PlayStation State of Play.

items.title: Wheel of Time AAA RPG Announced
items.summary: A full-fledged AAA video game adaptation of The Wheel of Time is in development by a studio behind the hit live-action series, potentially rivaling Game of Thrones games.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 announced a new Force of Law Warbond, giving players access to fresh content in the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Star Wars Eclipse Development Remains Very Slow
items.summary: Leaks indicate slow progress on Quantic Dream's Star Wars Eclipse, with the team awaiting funds tied to Spellcasters Chronicles' success, potentially years from completion.

items.title: Nintendo Sues Alleged Switch 2 Leaker
items.summary: Nintendo takes legal action against accessory maker Genki for allegedly misleading consumers and leaking Switch 2 information.

items.title: Blizzard Partners with Nexon for Overwatch Expansion in Korea
items.summary: Blizzard Entertainment teams up with Nexon to expand Overwatch in Korea, as covered in recent gaming news updates.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Wipro Acquires Mindsprint for $375M and Signs $1B+ Contract with Olam
items.summary: Indian IT giant Wipro has agreed to buy the IT services arm of Singapore-based Olam, Mindsprint, for $375 million and secured an eight-year contract worth over $1 billion with Olam. Wipro shares rose as much as 3.2% following the announcement.[1]

items.title: Jack Dorsey's Bitchat App Removed from China App Store
items.summary: Apple removed Jack Dorsey's Bluetooth P2P messaging app Bitchat from the App Store in China at the request of the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC); the app, launched last July, has been used in protests across five countries.[1]

items.title: Netflix Launches Kids' Games App Netflix Playground
items.summary: Netflix has released Netflix Playground, a dedicated games app for children aged eight and under, now available in the US, UK, Canada, Australia, the Philippines, and New Zealand.[1]

items.title: Samsung Discontinuing Its Texting App, Recommending Google Messages
items.summary: Samsung is shutting down its native texting app and directing affected users to switch to Google Messages as the default.[3]

items.title: Xoople Raises $130M Series B for Satellite Earth Data Constellation
items.summary: Xoople, developing a satellite constellation to gather earth data, secured $130 million in Series B funding, bringing total funding to $225 million.[1]
